key_insight: A Siamese GNN can learn subgroup relations between finite groups by combining Cayley-graph structure with algebraic features.
engineering_relevance: This offers a data-driven surrogate for computational group-theory tasks, potentially speeding up subgroup screening in algebra systems.
method: Represent each finite group as an undirected Cayley graph.
Encode both graphs with tied Siamese GNN branches to obtain embeddings.
Fuse graph embeddings with direct algebraic features and classify subgroup relations with a fully connected head.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 95.9% test accuracy (47/49) on an independent test set.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the chosen Cayley representation and the limited scale of the reported evaluation, so generalization beyond the test set should be checked carefully.
paper_title: Learning Subgroup Relations Using Siamese Graph Neural Networks
